description_long: This is a polygonal stone water well with a hexagonal or octagonal stone base, constructed from stacked, light brown stone blocks. Two tall, irregular wooden posts rise vertically from the base, supporting a horizontal wooden beam at the top. A simple rope and pulley system suspends a wooden bucket above the well opening, with the rope tied around the bucket and looped over the beam. The well's interior is open and hollow, allowing access to the water below. The structure is rustic and utilitarian, with no visible cover or decorative elements, and the materials appear weathered and natural.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden frame and suspended bucket, featuring a rope and pulley system and an open, hexagonal stone base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's open stone base, wooden posts, horizontal beam, and the bucket suspended by a rope above the opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone base and the alignment of the bucket and posts, with the bucket partially obscured by the post.
description_view_2: Alternate front view similar to view 0, with the bucket and rope system clearly visible and the wooden posts framing the well opening.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the wooden posts and beam, with the bucket mostly hidden behind the post and the stone base visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A visible rope and bucket mechanism is present, with the bucket suspended by a rope looped over a horizontal wooden beam.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low, hexagonal stone wall approximately 0.6 meters high.
